steps: Go to https://app.posthog.com and sign in to your account
Click your profile icon or navigate to 'Settings' in the left sidebar
Select 'Personal API keys' or go directly to https://app.posthog.com/settings/user-api-keys
Enter a label for the key (e.g. 'My Integration')
Select the scopes required for your use case
Copy the key immediately — it will never be shown again

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: Personal API Key
credential_fields.description: Your PostHog personal API key (not the project API key)

notes: Free open-source plan available; cloud plans start with a generous free tier
Personal API keys are for server-side use only — never expose in frontend code
The key is shown only once — copy it before leaving the page
PostHog also has a 'Project API Key' (write-only, for event capture) — this guide is for the personal API key (read/write for management APIs)
Self-hosted PostHog users: replace app.posthog.com with your instance URL
Most API endpoints require the project ID in the path: /api/projects/{project_id}/...

test_endpoint.description: List all projects in the organization
